description: Cross-binding pin for `validate_capabilities(caps)`. Each case carries a wire-format `caps` (tags + metadata) and the expected `errors` + `warnings` arrays a conformant validator emits. Bindings sort their output by canonical-JSON form (errors first, then warnings; each list sorted by JSON-stringified entry) before comparing; the fixture's `expected_*` arrays are already in that canonical sort order. See README.md.

cases.name: empty_caps_is_clean
cases.summary: Default `CapabilitySet` produces a clean report (no errors, no warnings).

cases.name: well_formed_hardware_axis_tags
cases.summary: Every key under `hardware.*` matches its declared `ValueType`.

cases.name: type_mismatch_number_with_non_numeric_value
cases.summary: `hardware.memory_mb` declared as Number; the value `lots` doesn't parse → TypeMismatch error.

cases.name: type_mismatch_presence_key_with_value
cases.summary: `hardware.gpu` is a Presence key — emitting it as `hardware.gpu=true` is a TypeMismatch (the Presence type forbids a value).

cases.name: type_mismatch_number_key_with_no_value
cases.summary: `hardware.memory_mb` is a Number key — emitting it as a bare `hardware.memory_mb` (no value) is a TypeMismatch with `actual: <no value>`.

cases.name: type_mismatch_bool_key_with_non_bool_value
cases.summary: `software.model.<i>.loaded` is a Bool key — only `true` / `false` parse.

cases.name: index_malformed_non_numeric_index
cases.summary: `software.model.<i>.<sub>` requires `<i>` to parse as `u32`; `bogus` doesn't → IndexMalformed.

cases.name: index_malformed_overflows_u32
cases.summary: N-5 regression: `software.model.<i>.<sub>` requires `<i>` to fit in `u32` (Rust `idx.parse::<u32>()`). `99999999999999` is all digits but overflows u32 → IndexMalformed. Pre-fix the Go reference impl accepted unbounded digit runs.

cases.name: type_mismatch_number_negative
cases.summary: N-5 regression: substrate `ValueType::Number` is unsigned u64 (Rust `value.parse::<u64>()`, CR-15). Negative values surface as TypeMismatch. Pre-fix the Go reference impl admitted leading `-` via an `isIntegerLiteral` helper.

cases.name: type_mismatch_number_exceeds_u64_max
cases.summary: N-5 regression: u64::MAX is 18446744073709551615; one more (`18446744073709551616`) is all digits but overflows. Rust `value.parse::<u64>()` rejects → TypeMismatch. Pre-fix the Go reference impl admitted unbounded digit runs.

cases.name: unknown_key_under_known_axis_is_warning
cases.summary: Forward-compat: a key the schema doesn't enumerate (`hardware.experimental_field`) produces an UnknownKey warning, not an error.

cases.name: unknown_sub_key_under_known_indexed_shape_is_warning
cases.summary: Forward-compat: a known `software.model.<i>.*` shape with an unknown sub-key produces UnknownKey on the FULL key path (`model.0.extra_field`) — pinned so consumers can correlate the warning back to the exact tag.

cases.name: legacy_tag_emits_warning
cases.summary: Untyped tags (no axis prefix, no reserved prefix) parse as `Tag::Legacy` and produce a LegacyTag warning.

cases.name: reserved_prefix_tag_passes_unchecked
cases.summary: Reserved-prefix tags (`scope:`, `causal:`, `fork-of:`, `heat:`) pass validation without errors or warnings — their body shape is application-defined.

cases.name: combined_errors_and_warnings_sort_canonically
cases.summary: Multiple violations across types — pinned to verify each binding's canonical sort order.
